data.title: C Link Squared FY 2025 Loss Of MYR165 Million Challenges Bullish Narratives
data.description:
C-Link Squared (SEHK:1463) has released its FY 2025 numbers with first half revenue of MYR55.5 million and a basic EPS loss of MYR0.0575 per share, setting a cautious tone for investors watching how the business is handling profitability pressure. Over the last three reported halves, revenue has moved from MYR49.6 million in 1H FY 2024 to MYR47.4 million in 2H FY 2024 and then to MYR55.5 million in 1H FY 2025. Over the same periods, net income has swung from a profit of MYR1.1 million to losses of MYR22.5 million and MYR165.3 million, signalling margins that remain firmly under strain.

See our full analysis for C-Link Squared.

With the headline numbers set, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the prevailing market narratives around C-Link Squared and where those stories might be challenged by the latest margin picture.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Losses Deepen To MYR182.1 Million On A TTM Basis

  • Over the last twelve months, C-Link Squared reported total revenue of MYR126.6 million and a net loss of MYR182.1 million, so the business is currently generating more in losses than in sales.
  • Critics highlight a bearish pattern of worsening profitability, and the trailing loss of MYR182.1 million alongside a five year profit decline of 88.1% a year strongly supports that concern.
    • On a half year view, net income moved from a loss of MYR22.5 million in 2H FY 2024 to a loss of MYR165.3 million in 1H FY 2025, which points to heavier pressure on margins rather than stabilisation.
    • Basic EPS over the latest twelve month periods shifted from a small loss of MYR0.00754 per share to a loss of MYR0.9641 per share, which lines up with the view that earnings quality is weak at present.

Revenue Holds Above MYR100 Million While Margins Stay Under Strain

  • The trailing twelve month revenue stepped from MYR96.99 million to MYR102.9 million and then to MYR126.6 million, yet net losses over those same snapshots widened from MYR21.4 million to MYR187.9 million and then MYR182.1 million.
  • What stands out against a more bullish narrative about business scale is that higher revenue has not translated into profit, because losses have remained large compared with the MYR126.6 million of sales.
    • At the half year level, 1H FY 2025 revenue of MYR55.5 million sits above 1H FY 2024 revenue of MYR49.6 million, while net income swung from a profit of MYR1.1 million to a loss of MYR165.3 million, so margin compression is the key issue rather than top line traction.
    • Basic EPS followed the same pattern, turning from a small profit of MYR0.000397 per share in 1H FY 2024 to a loss of MYR0.0575 per share in 1H FY 2025, which challenges any bullish view that scale alone is enough to improve per share outcomes.

P/S Of 3.6x Sits Between Industry And Peers

  • C-Link Squared trades on a P/S of 3.6x, which is higher than the wider Asian Professional Services industry average of 1.4x but below the peer group average of 4.6x.
  • Consensus style commentary often frames this as a mixed valuation signal, and the combination of a premium to the industry alongside trailing twelve month losses of MYR182.1 million and share price volatility means investors are paying more than the sector average while earnings remain in the red.
    • The current share price of HK$0.305, when set against unprofitable trailing twelve month earnings and an 88.1% annual decline in profits over five years, suggests the market is still assigning value despite the loss making record.
    • At the same time, sitting below the 4.6x peer P/S leaves room for investors to debate whether the 3.6x multiple reflects the company’s multi line business mix or ongoing concern about the scale of the MYR182.1 million trailing loss.

data.title: Exelon Promise Puts Customer Relief At Center Of Regulated Utility Story
data.description:
• Exelon (NasdaqGS:EXC), through its BGE subsidiary, has launched The Exelon Promise, a customer focused initiative targeting rising energy costs.
  • The program is designed to provide both immediate relief and longer term protections for customers facing higher utility bills.
  • The move marks a broader effort by Exelon’s largest regulated utility to respond to economic pressures on households and communities.

For investors watching Exelon at a share price of $49.02, The Exelon Promise adds a fresh angle to the story around regulated utilities and consumer affordability. The company has posted an 11.6% return year to date and 10.6% over the past year, with longer term returns of 31.0% over three years and 83.9% over five years. This initiative is part of how investors may think about the balance between earnings, regulation and customer outcomes for NasdaqGS:EXC.

The Exelon Promise may also matter beyond BGE, as other utilities face similar pressure around energy affordability and regulatory scrutiny. Investors and stakeholders are likely to focus on how Exelon implements the program, its reception from regulators and community groups, and whether it sets a reference point for customer support efforts across the sector.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Exelon by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Exelon.

We've flagged 2 risks for Exelon. See which could impact your investment.

The Exelon Promise positions BGE and Exelon as leaning into customer affordability at a time when higher bills can quickly turn into regulatory and political pushback. For a regulated utility, programs that offer immediate bill relief and longer term protections can help maintain public support for large grid investment plans, which are central to Exelon’s earnings story. At the same time, this type of customer-first initiative has to be funded, either through internal cost controls, rate design or external support, so investors will be watching how Exelon balances its already stretched financial profile, including flagged concerns around interest coverage and leverage, with the cost of these commitments.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Exelon Narrative

  • The Exelon Promise directly addresses affordability, which is a key condition for regulators to continue supporting the grid modernization and transmission investment pipeline highlighted in the existing narrative.
  • If customer relief measures compress allowed revenue or delay rate recovery, they could challenge assumptions about margin resilience and earnings stability that underpin the long term grid investment story.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on large load growth, transmission projects and policy support, while a program like The Exelon Promise could add an extra layer of customer and political expectations that may not be fully reflected in those assumptions.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Exelon to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that Exelon’s interest payments are not well covered by earnings, so any additional spending tied to customer programs could tighten financial flexibility.
  • ⚠️ A 3.43% dividend is not well covered by free cash flows, which may limit room to absorb the cost of enhanced protections if regulators are slow to approve recovery.
  • 🎁 Earnings grew by 12.5% over the past year and are forecast to grow 7.7% per year, suggesting the business has been able to support returns while investing in its network.
  • 🎁 Exelon is trading at a P/E of 18.1x, slightly below the US market at 18.3x, and is assessed as good value versus peers and industry, which can appeal to investors comparing large regulated utilities such as Duke Energy or Southern Company.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors should watch how regulators in Maryland respond to The Exelon Promise, particularly whether customer relief is paired with timely cost recovery and clear rules for future rate cases. The scale and design of the program, and whether similar offerings appear at other Exelon utilities, will be important for assessing earnings visibility and capital planning discipline. Progress on Exelon’s broader grid and transmission projects, together with any changes in its debt metrics and interest coverage, will help show whether the company can support both customer affordability and the investment program that underpins its long term business model.

Approach 1: Bavarian Nordic Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and then discounts them back to what they might be worth today. It is essentially asking what an investor might reasonably pay now for all the cash the company is estimated to generate in future.

Bavarian Nordic’s latest twelve month free cash flow is DKK 1,400.95m. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts have provided explicit projections out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year estimated at DKK 1,785m. Beyond the initial analyst horizon, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows, which are then discounted to reflect risk and the time value of money.

On this basis, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of DKK 486.06 per share. Compared with the recent share price of DKK 197.25, the DCF output suggests the shares trade at a 59.4% discount to that intrinsic estimate. This indicates that Bavarian Nordic appears undervalued on this cash flow view.

Approach 1: CrowdStrike Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value using a required rate of return.

For CrowdStrike Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$1.24b. Analyst estimates and further extrapolated projections by Simply Wall St point to free cash flow of around US$4.64b in the 2031 financial year, with intermediate annual projections stepping up between these points.

Bringing all of those projected cash flows back to today using the DCF method gives an estimated intrinsic value of about US$354.84 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$390.41, this implies the stock is around 10.0% overvalued according to this model.

Approach 2: CrowdStrike Holdings Price vs Sales

For profitable, revenue generating software companies, the P/S ratio is often a useful way to think about value because it ties the share price directly to the sales the business is bringing in, without being distorted by accounting items that can affect earnings.

Growth expectations and risk usually drive what feels like a normal or fair P/S multiple. Higher growth or perceived resilience often supports a higher ratio, while greater uncertainty or weaker growth points to a lower one.

data.title: Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings (SEHK:2521) Margin Compression Reinforces Cautious Narratives
data.description:
Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings (SEHK:2521) has kicked off FY 2025 with first half revenue of CN¥358.8 million and basic EPS of CN¥0.0045, setting a cautious tone after a stronger FY 2024 second half that delivered CN¥347.8 million in revenue and EPS of CN¥0.0238. The company has seen revenue move from CN¥325.8 million in the first half of FY 2024 to CN¥358.8 million in the latest half, while EPS shifted from CN¥0.0062 to CN¥0.0045 as profitability metrics absorbed the impact of a weaker margin profile and a one off loss. For investors, the key takeaway is that margins compressed over the last 12 months even as the top line held up, leaving the quality of earnings firmly in focus.

See our full analysis for Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to set these results against the dominant market narratives around Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings to see which views are reinforced and which come under pressure.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Weaken With 3.8% Net Margin

  • Over the last 12 months, Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings reported a 3.8% net profit margin, compared with 7.3% a year earlier, while trailing 12 month net income excluding extra items was CN¥26.5 million on CN¥694.9 million of revenue.
  • Critics highlight that this weaker margin profile and the fact that earnings over the most recent year were described as negative sit uncomfortably alongside a relatively modest 3.2% compound annual earnings growth rate over five years, which can be seen in tension with any bullish view that the business offers steadily compounding profits.
    • For a bullish stance that leans on stability in essential services, a move from a 7.3% net margin to 3.8% means profitability has been under pressure over the last year.
    • The CN¥26.5 million trailing 12 month net income result is well below the CN¥39.1 million booked in the second half of FY 2024 alone, which challenges the idea that recent profit levels form a firm base.

One Off CN¥24.4m Loss Distorts The Picture

  • The trailing 12 month figures include a one off loss of CN¥24.4 million, which has had a material impact on reported profitability metrics and helps explain why the latest trailing net margin sits at 3.8%.
  • What stands out for a more optimistic take is that some of the recent earnings weakness is tied to this single large charge rather than recurring operations. However, the bearish angle that margins are compressed still finds support in the step down from a 7.3% margin to 3.8%.
    • Bears can point to the CN¥7.9 million net income in the first half of FY 2025 versus CN¥39.1 million in the second half of FY 2024 as evidence that profit is currently running at a lower level, even before considering the one off charge.
    • More optimistic readers may instead separate the CN¥24.4 million one time hit from the underlying CN¥694.9 million of trailing revenue to assess whether the core business looks more stable than the headline margin suggests.

Premium 63.8x P/E And Volatile Share Price

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 63.8x, far above both the peer average of 12.2x and the Hong Kong Commercial Services industry average of 9x, while the share price of HK$0.99 has also been highly volatile over the past three months.
  • What is striking for a cautious, more bearish narrative is that this premium multiple and recent share price swings sit alongside compressed profitability, with the 3.8% net margin and negative year on year earnings growth raising questions about how comfortably the fundamentals support such a high P/E.
    • Bears argue that paying 63.8x trailing earnings when five year earnings growth has averaged only 3.2% per year, and the most recent year is described as loss making, leaves limited room for disappointment using the current numbers.
    • The combination of high valuation versus peers and recent share price volatility means any future shifts in reported margins or one off items could have an outsized impact on how the market prices the stock.

To see how other investors are weighing this high P/E against the recent margin pressure, you can read what the community is saying in more detail through the 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings.
